articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a specialty payments platform built specifically for the amusement industry. The deal signals growing investor confidence in FEC-focused fintech and could accelerate product development and market expansion for the platform. FEC operators should watch how this capital infusion affects the competitive landscape for cashless and integrated payment solutions.

articles.title: ROLLER Partners with Adyen to Offer Embedded Financing to 3,000+ Venues
articles.summary: ROLLER, a leading venue management software provider, has partnered with Adyen to give more than 3,000 venues access to fast embedded financing through Adyen Capital. The integration allows FEC operators to access growth capital directly within the ROLLER platform, streamlining the funding process. This is a significant development for operators seeking flexible financing options tied to their existing revenue data.

articles.title: Semnox Powers New Indoor FEC Funzy Playground with Cashless and Access Control Platform
articles.summary: Semnox Solutions has deployed its Parafait platform at Funzy Playground, a new indoor FEC inside a Romanian mall, supporting 85 arcade games with RFID cashless readers, time-based billing for play zones, and automated access control gates. The implementation demonstrates how a single integrated platform can handle diverse FEC operations from arcade revenue to trampoline zone management. Operators evaluating all-in-one technology solutions will find this a useful real-world case study.

articles.title: Valo Motion Launches Groundfall, a Standalone Mixed-Reality 'Floor Is Lava' Attraction
articles.summary: Valo Motion has announced Groundfall, a new standalone mixed-reality product based on its viral 'floor is lava' game that has amassed over 200 million social media views. Available in configurations for 4 to 10 players, the self-contained unit targets FECs seeking high-engagement, family-friendly attractions with strong social media appeal. The product will officially debut at IAAPA Expo Europe in London this September.

articles.title: Intercard to Showcase Award-Winning iQ QR Code Reader at IAAPA Expo Asia 2026
articles.summary: Intercard will present its iQ reader, which supports QR code-based cashless payments, at IAAPA Expo Asia in Hong Kong from June 10-12. The company's continued push into Asia-Pacific markets underscores growing international demand for cashless arcade technology. FEC operators and suppliers attending the show can evaluate the iQ reader firsthand at booth 1006.

articles.title: K1 Speed Opens Multi-Level Indoor Go-Kart Center in Thornton, Colorado
articles.summary: K1 Speed has opened a new indoor amusement center in Thornton, Colorado featuring a multi-level go-kart track. The expansion continues the brand's aggressive growth strategy and illustrates ongoing consumer demand for premium indoor karting experiences. Competing FEC operators in the Colorado market should take note of this new competitive entrant.

articles.title: Supercharged Entertainment Concept to Join Polaris Corridor Alongside Topgolf and Smash Park
articles.summary: A new Supercharged entertainment venue is planned for the Polaris area, adding to an already dense cluster of competitive social entertainment venues including Topgolf and Smash Park. The development highlights how destination entertainment districts are forming around anchor operators, intensifying competition in suburban markets. FEC developers and operators should monitor this corridor-clustering trend as it reshapes local market dynamics.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Takes Over Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has signed a lease for part of a former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, continuing the trend of FEC concepts repurposing dead retail square footage. This type of adaptive reuse has become a major growth driver for the FEC industry as mall owners seek experiential tenants to drive foot traffic. Developers and operators looking at mall-based expansion will find this a timely example.

articles.title: IP Strategy in Immersive Attractions: Why the Licensing Decision Shapes Everything
articles.summary: A detailed analysis of how the choice between licensed IP and original IP fundamentally shapes the structure, economics, and long-term operations of immersive entertainment projects. Licensed IP offers immediate audience recognition but introduces approval rights, usage restrictions, and ongoing royalty obligations that operators often underestimate at the outset. FEC and immersive experience developers considering IP partnerships should read this before entering license negotiations.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Opens New Venue in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: Thunderzone Family Entertainment has opened a new location in Carrollton, Georgia, expanding the FEC brand's footprint in the Southeast. New venue openings in smaller markets signal continued operator confidence in demand for out-of-home family entertainment outside major metros. Local operators and regional developers should track this expansion as an indicator of market viability in mid-sized cities.

articles.title: Spokane Valley FEC Flight 509 Closes Permanently After Two Years in Business
articles.summary: Flight 509, a family entertainment center in Spokane Valley, Washington, has permanently closed its doors after just two years of operation, drawing disappointment from local families ahead of summer. The closure is a cautionary data point for operators and investors evaluating FEC viability, particularly for newer concepts in secondary markets. Understanding the operational and financial challenges that led to this closure could provide valuable lessons for the broader industry.

articles.title: Mattel Wonder Indoor Waterpark Planned for Weldon Mills Resort Development
articles.summary: A Mattel Wonder-branded indoor waterpark is set to be part of the planned Weldon Mills Resort, adding a major licensed IP attraction to the development. The project reflects the growing trend of toy and entertainment IP brands extending into physical resort and waterpark experiences. FEC and waterpark operators should watch this development as Mattel continues to build its location-based entertainment portfolio.

articles.title: Gateway Ticketing's Galaxy Platform Goes Live at John Ball Zoo with Unified Admissions and Revenue System
articles.summary: John Ball Zoo in Grand Rapids has implemented Gateway Ticketing's Galaxy platform, integrating ticketing, retail, and food and beverage operations into a single unified system across its 103-acre campus. The RevGen solution gives the zoo a centralized view of admissions, membership, and ancillary revenue, enhancing operational efficiency and guest experience. While zoo-focused, the platform's capabilities are directly applicable to large FEC campuses managing multiple revenue streams.

articles.title: H. Betti Industries Appoints Michael Arnone as Senior Marketing Director
articles.summary: H. Betti Industries, a major amusement industry distributor based in New Jersey, has named Michael Arnone as its new Senior Director of Marketing, bringing over 20 years of brand strategy and digital transformation experience. The appointment signals a strategic push to strengthen the company's market presence and customer engagement in the amusement and FEC supply sector. Operators who work with H. Betti should expect potential changes in how the company communicates product offerings and support services.

articles.title: Slick City Indoor Slide Park Opening New Location in Milford This Summer
articles.summary: Slick City, the growing indoor slide park concept, is expanding to Milford with a new venue opening this summer. The brand's continued geographic expansion demonstrates strong consumer appetite for novel indoor active entertainment beyond traditional trampolines. FEC operators and investors tracking emerging active entertainment formats should note Slick City's accelerating growth.

articles.title: Universal Kids Resort Sets July 1 Opening Date in Frisco, Texas
articles.summary: Universal Kids Resort, the first Universal Destinations theme park designed exclusively for young children and families, will open on July 1, 2026 in Frisco, Texas, with tickets and annual passes now on sale. The 20-acre park features seven themed lands built around DreamWorks, Nickelodeon, and Illumination IP. While a large-scale theme park, its family-first positioning and regional location make it a significant competitive benchmark for FECs and family attractions in the Dallas-Fort Worth market.

articles.title: Dellshire Resort Opens in Wisconsin Dells with Fire-Breathing Dragon as Centerpiece
articles.summary: Dellshire Resort has opened in Wisconsin Dells, featuring a large fire-breathing dragon as its signature visual attraction and adding to the already competitive entertainment landscape of one of the Midwest's top family tourism destinations. The resort's experiential design approach underscores the importance of iconic, shareable anchor elements in driving guest interest. FEC designers and operators can draw inspiration from the venue's approach to creating memorable, differentiated experiences.

articles.title: Immotion Expands Immersive VR Edutainment Offerings to Science Centers and Museums
articles.summary: Immotion, which has deployed motion-based VR experiences at over 150 zoo and aquarium locations, is now targeting science centers and museums with its immersive edutainment platform. The company's 360-degree VR combined with motion seating extends dwell time and supports educational programming goals. FECs looking to add premium, low-footprint VR attractions with educational appeal may find Immotion's platform a viable option.

articles.title: Kids Bowl Free Program Returns at Kentucky and Southern Indiana Locations This Summer
articles.summary: Bowling centers across Kentucky and Southern Indiana are participating in the Kids Bowl Free summer program, offering free games to children to drive summer traffic and family visits. The program is a widely used demand-generation tool in the bowling industry, helping venues capture incremental family spending on food, rentals, and arcade play. Bowling center operators not yet enrolled in the program may want to evaluate its potential for boosting summer revenue.

articles.title: New Indoor Entertainment Concept Creates Differentiated Atmosphere Inside Mall Setting
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment space is being highlighted for its ability to create a distinct, immersive atmosphere within a traditional mall environment, standing out from standard retail offerings. Experiential tenants that transform the feel of a mall space are increasingly sought by landlords looking to drive foot traffic and dwell time. FEC operators considering mall locations can use this as a reference point for how design and atmosphere differentiate successful venues.

articles.title: London-Originated Social Entertainment Venue With Interactive Soccer and Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A new social entertainment venue featuring virtual soccer, foosball, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is expanding from London to Paris, highlighting the international growth of the competitive socializing format. The concept blends sport-themed interactive games with food and beverage in a format that has proven popular across European urban markets. FEC operators considering competitive socializing formats can look to this expansion as evidence of the model's cross-market viability.

articles.title: AAA Expo 2026 in Guangzhou Showcases Emerging Amusement Ride and Attraction Innovation
articles.summary: The Asia Amusement and Attraction Expo 2026 in Guangzhou drew international visitors across its massive exhibition halls, featuring go-karts, interactive rides, animatronic dinosaurs, bouncy castles, and a wide range of emerging amusement products. The show floor offered a firsthand look at the scale and variety of products being developed for the Asian and global amusement markets. FEC operators and buyers sourcing new attractions should take note of the innovation coming out of Asia's growing amusement manufacturing sector.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Indoor Entertainment Venue in Lucknow, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone, an indoor family entertainment brand, has opened a new location in Lucknow, expanding its presence in India's rapidly growing FEC market. The opening reflects accelerating investment in structured family entertainment venues across India's tier-two cities. International FEC operators and suppliers looking at emerging markets should monitor India's FEC sector growth trajectory.

articles.title: Southern California Families Driving Surge in 'Birthday Party' Venue Searches
articles.summary: New data highlights a significant increase in Southern California families searching for birthday party venues, presenting a strong demand signal for FECs and indoor entertainment operators in the region. Birthday party packages remain one of the highest-margin revenue streams for family entertainment centers, making this trend directly actionable for operators. Venues that optimize their birthday party offerings and online visibility stand to capture a meaningful share of this demand.

articles.title: WhiteWater Achieves Platinum Status in Canada's Best Managed Companies for Eighth Consecutive Year
articles.summary: WhiteWater, the world's largest waterpark designer and manufacturer, has earned Platinum Club membership in the Canada's Best Managed Companies program, recognizing eight consecutive years of outstanding performance in strategy, innovation, and financial results. The recognition reinforces WhiteWater's standing as a top-tier supplier in the waterpark and aquatic attractions space. For FEC operators planning aquatic or surf attraction expansions, WhiteWater's continued stability and investment in excellence is a positive signal.

articles.title: Disney Launches AccuWeather-Powered In-Park Weather App for Walt Disney World and Disneyland
articles.summary: Disney has introduced the Disney Weather Check app, powered by AccuWeather, providing guests with real-time, location-specific weather forecasts and alerts across its Florida and California parks. The app reflects how major operators are leveraging weather intelligence to improve both guest experience and operational decision-making. FEC operators with outdoor components, go-karts, or mini golf can draw lessons from Disney's approach to integrating weather data into the guest journey.

articles.title: The GAB Spot in Fort Walton Beach Draws Strong Local Community Following
articles.summary: The GAB Spot, a new entertainment venue in Fort Walton Beach, Florida, is generating strong local interest and repeat visits, offering a compelling example of community-focused FEC positioning in a smaller coastal market. The venue's appeal highlights how authentic local engagement and a welcoming atmosphere can be powerful differentiators for independent FEC operators. Smaller-market operators can look to this story as a model for building loyal local customer bases.

articles.title: Museum of Flight Installs 3-Acre Rooftop Solar Array in $4M Sustainability Project
articles.summary: Seattle's Museum of Flight is installing a three-acre solar array on its Aviation Pavilion roof, funded in part by a $1.2 million state clean energy grant, with the goal of meeting the West Campus's full energy needs. The project offers a template for large entertainment venue operators looking to reduce operating costs and carbon footprint through renewable energy investment. FEC operators with large building footprints should monitor available state and federal clean energy incentives as a pathway to meaningful long-term savings.
